unmapped_findings: The κ'-carbide precipitation sequence follows: γ → γ' + γ'' → γ' + L12(SRO) → γ' + κ'

Dislocation planar glide is the dominant deformation mechanism in austenitic Fe-Mn-Al-C steels regardless of SFE and aging conditions, attributed to glide plane softening associated with short-range ordering (SRO)
New deformation mechanisms proposed: Microband Induced Plasticity (MBIP), Dynamic Slip Band Refinement (DSBR), and Shear Band Induced Plasticity (SIP) for high SFE austenitic steels
Critical lattice constant for austenite matrix to precipitate intragranular κ'-carbide is about 0.3670 nm; below this no precipitation occurs
Composition boundary for intragranular κ' precipitation: 0.098(wt%Al) + 0.208(wt%C) = 1 - 0.0054(wt%Mn)
Austenite decomposition temperature decreases to about 386°C when Mn is increased to 30% in Fe-Mn-Al-C steels
Specific energy absorption at high strain rates (~10^3/s) is about 0.5 J/mm^3 for Al > 7%, which is 50% higher than conventional deep drawing steels (0.16-0.25 J/mm^3)
Corrosion resistance of Fe-Mn-Al-C alloys is far inferior to 304 stainless steel and comparable to conventional high strength steels

Hot crack susceptibility during welding is affected by weld morphology: Widmanstätten austenite (WA) and acicular austenite (AA) types show greater susceptibility than vermicular or lacy ferrite types
Formation of DO3 or B2 ordered phases in ferrite leads to embrittlement; Al content in ferritic steels should be limited to maximum 7% for automotive applications

Processing issues include: Al reactions with refractory materials, nozzle clogging by alumina, surface oxide formation during strip casting, and cracking during hot/cold forming due to ordered phases and grain boundary carbides

metadata.title: Current state of Fe-Mn-Al-C low density steels

process_sequence.others: finishing temperature; multiple reheating between passes may be applied to avoid cracking

process_sequence.cooling_medium: water, oil or other quench media
process_sequence.others: single austenite phase area; fast cooling to avoid intergranular κ-carbides
for precipitation hardening of κ'-carbides; or short aging at 600-900°C for 1-5 min for non-age hardenable grades

others.note: Hot rolled strips may be directly fast cooled to 500-750°C then slow cooled, or cooled to room temperature followed by isothermal annealing

structure.overall_structure: Four categories: ferritic (δ), ferrite-based duplex (δ+γ), austenite-based duplex (γ+δ), and austenitic (γ)

phases_present.crystal_structure: BCC (A2, B2, or DO3 depending on Al content)
phases_present.morphology: elongated bands or equiaxed grains after recrystallization

microstructure_list.defects: coarse κ-carbides along original δ-ferrite grain boundaries if slow cooled; ordered B2 or DO3 phases when Al > 10%

grain_structure.texture: Cube fibre {001}<110> with random components after DDRX

phases_present.crystal_structure: FCC (L12 ordered SRO or κ'-carbides may be present)
phases_present.grain_size: 8-135 μm depending on solution treatment time

microstructure_list.defects: short-range ordering (SRO) zones <2 nm; spinodal decomposition products; dislocation arrays

precipitates.distribution: homogeneous cuboidal particles within austenite grains; coherent
at grain boundaries or shear bands
precipitates.coherency: initially coherent with Kurdjumov-Sachs orientation relationship

phases_present.morphology: elongated stringers parallel to rolling direction
clustered particles or bands in ferrite matrix

microstructure_list.defects: banded microstructure; element partitioning between δ and γ

precipitates.distribution: at δ/γ interfaces or grain boundaries

microstructure_list.defects: B2 or DO3 ordering in ferrite; shear bands

defect_interaction.quality_description: glide plane softening leading to planar slip
shearing of cuboidal κ'-carbides by dislocations; precipitation strengthening
dislocation pile-up or bowing at B2 phase boundaries; Orowan looping
affects deformation twinning when M3C carbides form on grain boundaries

others.phase_boundary_character: κ'/γ interfaces coherent; α/κ interfaces semicoherent; B2/γ interfaces initially coherent then lose coherency upon coarsening

interface.phase_evolution: Spinodal decomposition: γ → γ' + γ'' → γ' + L12(SRO) → γ' + κ'; Eutectoid reaction: γ → α + κ + M23C6; Cellular transformation: γ → γ + α + κ

tensile_properties.youngs_modulus: 140-180 (depending on processing state and Al content; ~20% lower than HSLA steels)
tensile_properties.stress_strain_curves: continuous yielding in solution treated condition; discontinuous yielding after aging; horizontal or negative work hardening in highly alloyed or peak aged conditions

stress_strain_distribution.distribution: planar glide dominated; shear band formation at high strain rates

strain_hardening_rate.value: decreases with increasing Al content; inflection observed in TWIP-like behavior; flat or negative in peak aged high Al alloys

fatigue_properties.fatigue_limit: varies with aging condition; cyclic softening observed under strain amplitude 0.8%

creep_properties.rupture_life: lower in weldments than base metal at 600°C

density.value: decreases by 1.3% per 1 wt% Al addition; ferritic: 7.874-0.098(wt%Al); austenitic: 8.15-0.101(wt%Al)-0.41(wt%C)-0.0085(wt%Mn)

thermal_properties.thermal_conductivity: low thermal conductivity causes adiabatic shear band formation at high strain rates

corrosion_resistance.corrosion_rate: inferior to 304 stainless steel; comparable to conventional high strength steels
corrosion_resistance.passivation_behavior: Al addition does not provide significant improvement in corrosion resistance

oxidation_resistance.protective_scale: good oxidation resistance at elevated temperatures reported

service_conditions.environment: automotive structural applications; cryogenic applications potential

comparative_performance.benchmark_alloy: Mn-TWIP steels, HSLA steels, Cr-Ni stainless steels
comparative_performance.improvement_percentage: specific energy absorption ~50% higher than conventional deep drawing steels; density reduction ~17% for 12% Al addition

characterization_methods.purpose: microstructural observation; SRO detection; κ-carbide characterization; dislocation structure analysis
characterization_methods.key_findings: κ'-carbides coherent with matrix; SRO zones <2nm; planar dislocation arrays
shear band formation; deformation twinning; crack initiation sites
L12 superlattice reflections indicating SRO; κ-carbide peaks

characterization_methods.purpose: phase diagram calculation; phase fraction prediction
characterization_methods.key_findings: γ region expands to higher Al with Mn up to 20% then shrinks; κ phase forms when Al > 2% in Fe-Al-C or >5% in Fe-Mn-Al-C
off-stoichiometric composition with depletion of C and Al compared to ideal (Fe,Mn)3AlC

characterization_methods.purpose: high strain rate properties; Young's modulus measurement
characterization_methods.key_findings: E modulus decreases with Al addition; specific energy absorption ~0.5 J/mm^3 at 10^3/s

computational_details.simulation_method: thermodynamic equilibrium calculations; ab initio simulation
computational_details.validation_against_experiment: experimental data show γ region extends to higher Al with increasing Mn, but calculations show expansion only up to 20% Mn then shrinkage; deviation noted at high Mn and Al contents

analytical_model.equation_role: predict yield strength and UTS based on austenitic grain size
analytical_model.parameters: σy0 = 288 MPa, K = 461 MPa·μm^1/2 for YS; σuts0 = 742 MPa, K = 351 MPa·μm^1/2 for UTS
analytical_model.model_note: applicable to non-age hardenable Fe-Mn-Al-C alloys

analytical_model.parameters: SFE increases by 10 mJ/m^2 per wt% Al and 40 mJ/m^2 per wt% C; Mn decreases SFE in 0-15% range then increases
analytical_model.model_note: critical for predicting deformation mechanisms (TRIP/TWIP/MBIP transition)
